By Sila Ozeren Hacioglu, Security Research Engineer at Picus Security.

It’s a story the security community knows well. You bring in a shiny new automated penetration testing tool, and the first "run" is a revelation. The dashboard lights up with critical findings, lateral movement paths you didn't know existed, and a "Gotcha!" moment involving a legacy service account.

The Red Team feels like they’ve found a force multiplier; the CISO feels like they’ve finally automated the "human element" of security.

But then, the honeymoon ends.

On average, by the fourth or fifth execution, the "new" findings dry up. The tool starts reporting the same stale issues, and the once-shiny dashboard becomes just another screen delivering noise. This isn't just a lull in activity; it's the Validation Gap – the widening distance between what organizations actually validate and what they report as validated.

If you’ve started to feel like your automated pentesting tool is overpromising and underdelivering, you’re experiencing a shift in the market. The industry is waking up to the fact that while automated pentesting is a powerful feature, it’s an increasingly dangerous strategy when used in isolation.

The POC Cliff: Where Discovery Goes to Die

This pattern of exciting first run with significantly diminishing returns by run four, isn’t anecdotal.

Security practitioners call it the Proof-of-Concept (PoC) Cliff: the steep drop in new findings volume once the tool has exhausted its fixed scope. It’s not a tuning problem.

By design, automated pentesting solutions deliver their best results in the first run. Within a few cycles, exploitable paths within their scope are exhausted. But that doesn’t mean your environment is secure. It just means the tool has reached its limits, while deeper issues remain untested.

This is the structural ceiling of a tool operating against a deterministic surface. It’s an architectural limitation, not an operational one.

Automated pentesting chains its steps. Step B depends on Step A, and Step C depends on Step B. Once you patch the specific path the tool favors, it's blocked at Step A, and Steps B through Z never execute. The tool might be able to test 20 lateral movement techniques, but if it gets caught early in the chain, those techniques stay dark. You get the false sense of "mission accomplished" while the rest of your attack surface remains unprobed.

This is where Breach and Attack Simulation (BAS) draws a hard line. 

BAS doesn't chain; it runs thousands of independent, atomic simulations. Each technique gets its own clean execution. A blocked exfiltration test over DNS doesn't prevent testing exfiltration over HTTPS next. A failed lateral movement technique doesn't stop the tool from testing 19 others. 

One tests the path. The other tests the shield.

One Tool Finds the Path. Picus Tests the Rest.

Automated pentesting maps attack paths. Picus validates the other five surfaces: detection rules, prevention controls, identity, cloud, and AI.

Findings from your existing tools get normalized into a single prioritized queue. No rip and replace. See it live. 

Request a Demo

Clearing the Air: BAS vs. Automated Pentesting

To better understand the “why” of the PoC Cliff, we need to address a growing point of confusion in the industry. While Breach and Attack Simulation (BAS) and automated penetration testing share the broad goal of validation, they use different methods to answer different questions.

Think of BAS as a series of independent measurements. It continuously and safely emulates adversarial techniques, malware payloads, lateral movement, and exfiltration, to verify if your specific security controls (firewalls, WAF, EDR, SIEM) are actually doing their jobs.

Its primary mission is to test if your defenses are blocking or alerting on known threat behaviors. Each test stands alone as a check of your defensive strength.

Automated Penetration Testing, by contrast, is directional. It takes a more surgical, adversarial approach by chaining vulnerabilities and misconfigurations together the way a real attacker would. It excels at exposing complex attack paths, such as Kerberoasting in Active Directory or escalating privileges to reach a Domain Admin account. 

Though both are often thought of as “validation methods,” the  two are fundamentally different in mission and outcomes. One tells you how strong your individual defenses are; the other tells you how far an attacker can travel in spite of them.

The "Simplicity" Trap: Why Pentesting Isn't BAS

Recently, some vendors have proposed the idea that automated pentesting can, and should, replace BAS. On paper, it sounds great.

In reality, this isn't an upgrade; it’s a coverage regression disguised as a simplification.

As we’ve just seen, automated pentesting and BAS tools answer fundamentally different questions. To secure a modern enterprise, you need the answers to both:

  • BAS asks: "Are my firewalls, EDRs, WAFs, and SIEMs actually doing their jobs across the entire MITRE ATT&CK framework?" It focuses on the effectiveness of your defensive controls.

  • Automated Pentesting asks: "Can an attacker get from Point A to Point B using known exploits?" It focuses on the success of specific attack paths.

If you swap BAS assessments for automated pentesting, you stop validating your prevention and detection stack.

You might know that an attacker can’t reach your database via one specific exploit, but you have zero visibility into whether your EDR would even blink if they tried a different, non-exploitative technique.

The Six Blind Spots of the Modern Attack Surface

While marketing materials promise "comprehensive" coverage, the reality is that automated pentesting typically only scratches the surface of infrastructure and application paths. 

As shown above, two surfaces get no coverage from automated pentesting. Four get partial coverage at best. Not a single surface is fully covered. That's 0 for 6 completely validated. This creates a massive validation gap where today’s breaches are actually happening:

  1. Network & Endpoint Controls: Exploit paths are identified, but there is no confirmation if firewalls, WAF, IPS, DLP, or EDR are actually blocking the threats they’re configured to stop. Controls fail silently, and "configured" is mistakenly equated with "effective."

  2. Detection & Response Stack: Automated pentesting has no visibility into whether SIEM rules and EDR detection logic actually fire. The tool runs as the attacker, it cannot observe the defender. Detection coverage is assumed, not measured.

  3. Infrastructure & Application Attack Paths: These tests often hit a "POC cliff." While infrastructure paths are mapped, complex application-layer attack chains vary in coverage and often stay open and available to adversaries.

  4. Identity & Privilege: Existing paths are traversed, but there is no systematic validation of Active Directory configurations, IAM policies, and privilege boundaries.

  5. Cloud & Container Environments: Dynamic Kubernetes policies and cloud security controls frequently remain dark and un-revalidated as configurations drift.

  6. AI & Emerging Technology: Critical guardrails for internal LLMs against jailbreaks, prompt injection, and adversarial manipulation remain completely unvalidated.

The Intelligence Layer: Exposure Validation & Prioritization 

This cross-cutting layer unifies these silos. Matching theoretical CVEs against live security control performance strips out noise, turning the 60%+ of findings falsely classified as high or critical down to the ~10% that are genuinely exploitable, reducing false urgency by over 80%, to produce one defensible, prioritized action list.

The Three Questions You Need to Ask

Understanding this gap is one thing; fixing it requires holding your validation vendors to a higher standard. To cut through the marketing hype and find out what a tool actually delivers, everything distills down to three fundamental diagnostic questions.

Bring them with you to every vendor meeting, every renewal conversation, and every budget review. They work because they are structural, not subjective. Any tool that answers all three with specificity and evidence deserves serious evaluation; any tool that cannot has just shown you where your gap is.

  1. Which of my six validation surfaces does your tool cover, and at what scope within each?

  2. How does your platform distinguish exploitable vulnerabilities from theoretical ones, specifically using my live security control performance data?

  3. How does your platform normalize findings from my other tools into a single, deduplicated, prioritized view and action list?

The difference between "we chose not to validate this surface" and "we didn't realize it wasn't being validated" is the difference between risk management and exposure. 

The Bottom Line

Your attack surface doesn't care which vendor's logo is on the tool. 

It only cares whether it has been tested. If your current automated pentesting deployment is leaving critical surfaces in the dark, it's time to remap your strategy. 

Our latest practitioner’s guide, The Validation Gap: What Automated Pentesting Alone Cannot See, provides the complete diagnostic framework you’ll need to audit your own coverage, diagnose where your coverage plateaus, and build a unified validation architecture. 

Start with the six surfaces. Score your own coverage. Knowing where your tools stop is how you decide where to go next.

Sponsored and written by Picus Security.

data.important_content: By Sila Ozeren Hacioglu , Security Research Engineer at Picus Security. It’s a story the security community knows well. You bring in a shiny new automated penetration testing tool, and the first "run" is a revelation. The dashboard lights up with critical findings, lateral movement paths you didn't know existed, and a "Gotcha!" moment involving a legacy service account. The Red Team feels like they’ve found a force multiplier; the CISO feels like they’ve finally automated the "human element" of security. But then, the honeymoon ends . On average, by the fourth or fifth execution, the "new" findings dry up. The tool starts reporting the same stale issues, and the once-shiny dashboard becomes just another screen delivering noise. This isn't just a lull in activity; it's the Validation Gap – the widening distance between what organizations actually validate and what they report as validated . If you’ve started to feel
data.title: Why Your Automated Pentesting Tool Just Hit a Wall

data.content: When you’re looking to cut down on monthly subscriptions, cloud storage is an easy place to start. FileJump’s lifetime plan gives you ongoing access to 2TB of secure storage through a simple web interface that works across desktop and mobile. For new users, it’s priced at $89.00, which comes out lower than what many services charge per year. The platform sticks to the essentials you’ll actually use: drag-and-drop uploads, clean folder organization, and fast previews for images, videos, PDFs, spreadsheets, and presentations. That means you can quickly check files without constantly downloading them. Sharing is just as straightforward. Send links to files or folders, invite others to collaborate with free accounts, and bypass download limits. Security is supported with AES encryption, helping keep your files protected while still accessible across your devices. The plan also allows uploads up to 15GB per file, so you can store large videos , full project folders, or raw files without breaking them apart. With progressive web apps available on desktop and mobile, they run like native apps without taking up extra space. For most people, 2TB is more than enough room — think entire photo collections, years of documents, and ongoing projects all stored in one place. Sharing files is simple, whether you’re sending a quick link or granting folder access without dealing with email attachments or version confusion. Since it’s a one-time purchase for new users, you won’t have to think about renewals or unexpected storage fees later. However, it should be noted that while this is a lifetime deal, if the company goes out of business then it would not longer be effective. So, only purchase this deal if you're willing to take that risk. Get a lifetime subscription to FileJump 2TB cloud storage for $89.00. Prices subject to change. data.content: Experimenting with AI has lost its sparkle, especially as subscriptions to different models have started to stack up. One tool charges $20 a month, another $30, but you need both for specific tasks. 1min.AI simplifies workflows by offering lifetime access to leading AI models, including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and more on a single platform for one flat price. It acts as your creative partner, workflow assistant, and design hub all in one platform. The Advanced Business Plan includes a high monthly credit allowance, giving you the ability to generate over a million words, create thousands of images, produce dozens of videos, and more on a consistent basis. It also supports sharing with up to 20 users, making it useful for teams. All of this is available for a one-time payment of $99.99 (reg. $540). Whether you need to draft a blog post, rewrite emails, or fine-tune your brand voice, AI tools inside the platform can help streamline the process. Think of it like having a digital team ready to assist with a wide range of everyday tasks. Beyond writing, 1min.AI includes powerful image tools that let you generate visuals, edit designs, and enhance photos quickly. You can create graphics, remove backgrounds, or upscale images for sharper results without switching between different apps. It also offers tools to help manage and edit PDFs. Its audio and video capabilities add another layer of flexibility. You can translate, convert, or improve audio files and produce video content without needing separate software. Everything is accessible through a user-friendly interface that makes it easy to get started right away. And since it’s a lifetime purchase, you won’t need to worry about ongoing subscription costs. The platform also continues to improve over time, with regular updates and new features added to keep up with the latest in AI technology. data.content: Arduino and IoT projects are easier to grasp when you’re actually building them. This bundle focuses on hands-on learning, guiding you through real-world systems instead of just theory. The Complete Arduino, Raspberry Pi & Smart Home IoT Bundle is built to close that gap, combining multiple project-based courses into one structured path for $49.99. Instead of isolated lessons, it walks through practical builds like smart alarm systems, weather stations, robotics, and home automation setups. The bundle starts with core Arduino fundamentals — including working with the Arduino UNO, IDE setup, breadboards, and communication protocols like SPI and I2C — then expands into more applied projects. Trade tutorials for tangible builds: Automated plant monitoring with sensors and irrigation control A self-balancing robot using gyroscopes and motor control Smart home automation using ESP32, MQTT, and Home Assistant A full weather station with real-time environmental data Security systems with proximity sensors and alerts It also goes deeper into embedded systems and networking fundamentals, covering circuit design, wireless communication, and IoT integration . The progression makes it accessible for beginners but still useful if you want to move into more advanced builds. Everything is self-paced with lifetime access, so you can move between foundational lessons and more complex projects without rushing through material or paying for multiple platforms. Ready to start building instead of just learning? This Arduino, Raspberry Pi, and Smart Home IoT bundle is available now for $49.99 and includes lifetime access to the full course library. Arduino and IoT projects are easier to grasp when you’re actually building them. This bundle focuses on hands-on learning, guiding you through real-world systems instead of just theory.

The Complete Arduino, Raspberry Pi & Smart Home IoT Bundle is built to close that gap, combining multiple project-based courses into one structured path for $49.99. Instead of isolated lessons, it walks through practical builds like smart alarm systems, weather stations, robotics, and home automation setups.

The bundle starts with core Arduino fundamentals — including working with the Arduino UNO, IDE setup, breadboards, and communication protocols like SPI and I2C — then expands into more applied projects.

Trade tutorials for tangible builds:

  • Automated plant monitoring with sensors and irrigation control
  • A self-balancing robot using gyroscopes and motor control
  • Smart home automation using ESP32, MQTT, and Home Assistant
  • A full weather station with real-time environmental data
  • Security systems with proximity sensors and alerts

It also goes deeper into embedded systems and networking fundamentals, covering circuit design, wireless communication, and IoT integration. The progression makes it accessible for beginners but still useful if you want to move into more advanced builds.

Everything is self-paced with lifetime access, so you can move between foundational lessons and more complex projects without rushing through material or paying for multiple platforms.

Ready to start building instead of just learning? This Arduino, Raspberry Pi, and Smart Home IoT bundle is available now for $49.99 and includes lifetime access to the full course library.

data.content: Keeping your skill set current has always mattered, but the pace of change right now is on another level. Artificial intelligence has gone from something most people were curious about to something many rely on daily, and those who understand how to use it are already pulling ahead. The ChatGPT and Automation E-Degree is designed to help you catch up and stay competitive, offering a practical introduction to how AI can fit into real work scenarios — and, right now, it’s available for $19.99 (reg. $790). Learn how to automate everyday tasks This bundle includes 12 courses totaling more than 25 hours of content, all accessible on your own schedule. Once you’re enrolled, you can move through lessons at your own pace and revisit them whenever you need a refresher, which makes it easy to build skills without disrupting your routine. The content is focused on real-world applications rather than theory. You’ll learn how to customize ChatGPT for different use cases, set up automations that cut down on repetitive work, and turn raw data into clear, usable insights. Along the way, you’ll also explore more than 20 additional AI tools that can support everything from marketing workflows to coding projects and day-to-day business operations. It’s built with beginners in mind, so you don’t need any technical experience to get started. The lessons break down how AI can actually be used in everyday work, whether that means drafting content faster, organizing large amounts of information, or improving how you collaborate with others. As you progress, you’ll start to see how small automations can add up to meaningful time savings. By the end of the program, you’ll have a clearer understanding of how to use AI as a practical tool, not just a trend, along with a certificate of completion you can use to showcase your new skills to employers or clients. For a limited time, you can get the full ChatGPT and Automation course bundle for just $19.99. Buying this deal? You can also score a lifetime license to Microsoft Office 2021 for free—just add it to your cart and apply code GWP4WIND (for Windows) or GWP4MAC (for Mac) at checkout through 4/19. data.content: With aggressive ads and growing privacy concerns, a clean, safe experience is harder to come by — especially across multiple devices. The AdGuard Family Plan tackles all of that at once, permanently covering up to nine devices for a one-time $15.97 (MSRP $169.99) through April 12. Instead of relying on basic browser extensions, it works at a deeper level to block ads before they even load, which can noticeably improve page speed and reduce clutter. The ad-blocking goes beyond banners and pop-ups. It also filters out video ads, intrusive overlays, and tracking scripts that follow your activity across sites. That means fewer interruptions and more control over your data without constantly tweaking settings. Security is another layer here. It’s also flexible enough to work across Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android, so you’re not locked into a single ecosystem. Once it’s set up, it syncs across devices and keeps working without ongoing fees or renewals. Instead of stacking multiple apps to block ads, protect privacy, and filter content , AdGuard wraps everything into one straightforward solution. data.content: Cybersecurity isn’t just a specialized field anymore—it’s becoming a core part of how modern businesses operate. For professionals looking to move into security roles or deepen their expertise, structured training can make a meaningful difference. The CISSP Security & Risk Management Training Bundle can help, and lets you access courses whenever it suits your schedule. It’s currently on sale for $19.99 (MSRP $424). This bundle is designed to align with the Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP) certification, one of the more recognized credentials in the industry. It covers all eight domains required for the exam, from security and risk management to software development security. That breadth makes it useful not only for exam prep, but also for understanding how security fits into broader IT and business operations. The course content focuses on practical areas like risk assessment, network security, identity management, and incident response. These are skills that show up across roles—from security analysts and consultants to leadership positions like security managers or CISOs. Even for those not pursuing certification immediately, the material in this security and risk management training bundle provides a structured overview of how organizations approach security challenges. With 8 courses and 21 hours of content, the bundle offers a compact way to cover a wide range of topics without needing to piece together multiple resources. It’s particularly relevant for IT professionals looking to pivot into security or expand their responsibilities in an existing role. The bundle lowers the barrier to entry for CISSP-focused training, making it easier to explore or advance in cybersecurity without a significant upfront investment. Access this CISSP Security & Risk Management Training Bundle for life for a one-time $19.99 (MSRP $424). Prices subject to change. data.content: In a crowded field like cybersecurity, it can be difficult to stand out. For aspiring professionals who want a way to get ahead, industry certifications are a difficult but worthwhile path. Prepping for the exams can be costly, but the Complete CompTIA and IT Exam Training Bundle can make it more manageable, and it’s on sale for $39.99 (reg. $2,748.75). Tech training for multiple certifications This bundle brings together several kinds of training instead of treating certification prep like a single textbook with a few quizzes attached. CramWise focuses on exam simulators, practice tests, and performance-based questions, so you can work through the same kinds of tasks that tend to make real certification exams harder than people expect. That matters for anyone studying for CompTIA, Cisco, or AWS paths, because passing usually depends on more than memorizing definitions. You need to recognize scenarios, make decisions under pressure, and understand how technical concepts actually show up in practice. DojoLab adds guided labs and hands-on challenges aligned with official CompTIA objectives across A+, Network+, Security+, and Linux+. LinuxPath goes deeper into Linux with command-line training, system administration concepts, and practical exercises that help make core tasks feel less abstract. CodeDirect shifts into Python and teaches the language through projects, so beginners can move past syntax drills and start building something useful. ExamsDigest rounds out the package with access to more certification paths across AWS, CompTIA, Cisco, Microsoft, and Google. That mix makes this bundle useful for more than one kind of learner. Someone new to IT can use it to build a foundation, pick a certification path, and get comfortable with labs and simulated exams. Someone already in tech can use it to fill skill gaps, prep for a promotion, or add another credential without buying a separate course every time. All course materials are yours for life with no recurring costs of any kind. Right now, it’s only $39.99 to get the Complete CompTIA and IT Training Bundle . Prices subject to change. data.content: PDF tools have a reputation problem. Adobe Acrobat is the industry standard, but its subscription pricing puts it out of reach for plenty of users who just need reliable editing and conversion without a recurring bill. UPDF is a capable cross-platform PDF-management solution, and a lifetime license is currently available for $69.99, down from $149.99. UPDF 2.0 launched in 2025 with a redesigned interface and a full toolkit that covers most of what everyday users actually need from a PDF editor. The software runs on Windows, Mac, iOS, and Android, and one license covers up to four devices, including two desktops and two mobile devices. That kind of cross-platform flexibility is rare at this price point. Top-tier PDF editing On the editing side, UPDF lets users modify text properties, swap out images, add or remove links, and handle page-level organization tasks like inserting, splitting, rotating, and reordering pages. The annotation toolkit is equally thorough, with support for highlights, sticky notes, shapes, stamps, and freehand drawing. For anyone handling sensitive documents, built-in redaction and password protection tools are also included. Conversion is a strong suit as well. UPDF can output to Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and a range of image and text formats. The OCR engine supports 38 languages, which makes it a practical option for anyone working with scanned documents or non-English content. Fillable form creation and digital signature support round out the core feature set. One thing to flag: the AI assistant features, powered by GPT-4.1 and DeepSeek R1, are not bundled into this deal and require a separate purchase at updf.com. The lifetime license does include 2GB of UPDF Cloud storage for file syncing across devices, along with all future software updates at no added cost. Don’t miss your chance to grab this the UPDF PDF editor, which is rated 4.2 on the App Store. Pick up a lifetime license for UPDF for $69.99 (reg. $149.99) today. Prices subject to change. data.content: Knowing a second language is a useful skill, whether you’re traveling or just expanding what you can offer to a new job. However, learning a new language is difficult, and many language learning apps aren’t making it any easier by throwing long, niche grammar lessons at you. If you want a more practical way to learn a language, Babbel is now offering a lifetime subscription to all their language lessons for $159 (down from $646.20). What makes this language-learning app different? A lifetime subscription to Babbel gives you access to all 14 current languages, including Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, and many more. You can stick with one language or bounce around if your interests change. Babbel works across desktop and mobile, and your progress syncs across devices, so you can start a lesson on your laptop and finish it on your phone later. One of the most useful things Babbel does is keep lessons short, typically around 10 or 15 minutes so you can fit a few throughout the day. The lessons cover useful topics like transportation, dining, shopping, directions, business, and everyday conversation. That practical structure makes Babbel feel more grounded than apps that spend too much time on obscure vocabulary you’ll never need. Speech recognition also helps with pronunciation, and the AI conversation partner gives you a way to practice expressing yourself in real time instead of only tapping through flashcards. Personalized review sessions help reinforce what you’ve already learned, and offline access makes it easier to keep practicing when you don’t have Wi-Fi. Just download lessons ahead of time and keep going. What language do you want to learn first? Use code LEARN to get a Babbel lifetime subscription for $159. Prices subject to change. Knowing a second language is a useful skill, whether you’re traveling or just expanding what you can offer to a new job. However, learning a new language is difficult, and many language learning apps aren’t making it any easier by throwing long, niche grammar lessons at you.

If you want a more practical way to learn a language, Babbel is now offering a lifetime subscription to all their language lessons for $159 (down from $646.20).

What makes this language-learning app different?

A lifetime subscription to Babbel gives you access to all 14 current languages, including Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, and many more. You can stick with one language or bounce around if your interests change. Babbel works across desktop and mobile, and your progress syncs across devices, so you can start a lesson on your laptop and finish it on your phone later.

One of the most useful things Babbel does is keep lessons short, typically around 10 or 15 minutes so you can fit a few throughout the day. The lessons cover useful topics like transportation, dining, shopping, directions, business, and everyday conversation. That practical structure makes Babbel feel more grounded than apps that spend too much time on obscure vocabulary you’ll never need. Speech recognition also helps with pronunciation, and the AI conversation partner gives you a way to practice expressing yourself in real time instead of only tapping through flashcards.

Personalized review sessions help reinforce what you’ve already learned, and offline access makes it easier to keep practicing when you don’t have Wi-Fi. Just download lessons ahead of time and keep going.

What language do you want to learn first?

Use code LEARN to get a Babbel lifetime subscription for $159.

Prices subject to change.

data.content: Trying to learn a new language usually starts with good intentions and ends with abandoned apps, forgotten vocabulary, or expensive classes that don’t fit your schedule. The bigger problem isn’t motivation — it’s finding a system that actually works in real life, whether you’ve got five minutes or an hour. Mondly Premium is designed to solve that, giving you lifetime access for $79.97 (MSRP $299.99) through April 12. Instead of rigid lessons or memorization-heavy drills, it focuses on practical, daily use with short sessions that build naturally over time. You’re not limited to one language or one level, either. The platform includes 41 languages and adapts whether you’re starting from scratch or brushing up on something you already know. Lessons cover real-life situations like travel, work, and everyday conversations, so you’re learning what you’ll actually use instead of disconnected phrases. What sets it apart is how interactive it feels. Speech recognition lets you practice pronunciation in real time, while chatbot-style conversations simulate actual dialogue instead of passive learning. There are also thousands of daily lessons, weekly quizzes, and monthly challenges to help you stay consistent without feeling repetitive. Mondly also leans into flexibility. You can switch between devices, learn hands-free while multitasking, and track your progress with built-in testing tools backed by Pearson. Grammar support, vocabulary builders, and conjugation tables fill in the gaps so you’re not guessing your way through sentences. Instead of committing to another subscription or class schedule, this is a one-time purchase that keeps everything in one place — and keeps you moving forward. Get this lifetime subscription to Mondly Premium (all languages) for $79.97 (MSRP $299.99) before April 12. Prices subject to change. The bigger problem isn’t motivation — it’s finding a system that actually works in real life, whether you’ve got five minutes or an hour.

Mondly Premium is designed to solve that, giving you lifetime access for $79.97 (MSRP $299.99) through April 12. Instead of rigid lessons or memorization-heavy drills, it focuses on practical, daily use with short sessions that build naturally over time.

You’re not limited to one language or one level, either. The platform includes 41 languages and adapts whether you’re starting from scratch or brushing up on something you already know. Lessons cover real-life situations like travel, work, and everyday conversations, so you’re learning what you’ll actually use instead of disconnected phrases.

What sets it apart is how interactive it feels. Speech recognition lets you practice pronunciation in real time, while chatbot-style conversations simulate actual dialogue instead of passive learning. There are also thousands of daily lessons, weekly quizzes, and monthly challenges to help you stay consistent without feeling repetitive.

Mondly also leans into flexibility. You can switch between devices, learn hands-free while multitasking, and track your progress with built-in testing tools backed by Pearson.
